criteria: Implement Get/Set/Delete operations with O(1) average time complexity
Implement LRU eviction using hashmap + doubly linked list structure
Implement LFU eviction with frequency buckets for O(1) minimum frequency lookup
Support configurable eviction policy (LRU or LFU) at cache initialization
Implement TTL-based expiration with lazy eviction on access
Implement background cleanup goroutine for expired entries
Ensure thread-safe operations using sync.RWMutex (or sharded locks)
Enforce memory limit and evict entries when capacity exceeded
Track and expose cache metrics (hits, misses, evictions, size)
Handle size estimation for entries including key, value, and metadata overhead
Support concurrent access from multiple goroutines without deadlock or race conditions
Prioritize expired entry eviction over policy-based eviction when making space
Implement consistent hash ring with configurable virtual nodes per server (minimum 40 per weight unit)
Achieve key distribution within 10% variance across nodes with equal weights
Node addition moves only ~1/N keys (tested with 10,000+ sample keys, within 5% of expected)
Node removal handled gracefully with proper cleanup of virtual nodes and index maps
Client-side sharding with connection pooling per node
Support weighted nodes where key allocation is proportional to weight
Configurable hash function with FNV-1a default, interface for MurmurHash3 or custom
Binary search lookup for O(log V) where V = total virtual nodes
Thread-safe ring operations with RWMutex for concurrent reads
GetNodes(key, n) returns n distinct physical nodes for replication support
Proper error handling for empty ring, duplicate node IDs, and insufficient nodes
Configurable replication factor (N replicas per key) with support for 1-10 replicas
Primary-secondary replication model where all writes go to primary node
Asynchronous replication to secondaries with configurable sync timeout
Read from any replica with eventual consistency guarantees
Quorum-based automatic failover when primary fails (majority required)
Secondary promotion selects replica with most recent sync timestamp
Phi accrual or heartbeat-based failure detection with configurable timeouts
Lease-based leadership to prevent split-brain during network partitions
Graduated health states: Healthy → Suspect → Unhealthy → Dead
Read consistency levels: PRIMARY_ONLY, PRIMARY_PREFERRED, ANY
Replica lag tracking via LastSync timestamps
Jittered reconnection to prevent thundering herd on node recovery
Broadcast primary changes to all cluster nodes after failover
Configurable failure timeout and heartbeat interval
Metrics: replica lag, failover count, quorum availability, heartbeat latency
